gates.requirement: Exact PR #29 accepted head, pre-merge main, merge commit, parent order, and merge-tree identity are verified.
The observed pre-closeout relation is origin/main ahead of origin/v1.4.0-development by exactly one merge commit.
The working tree is clean before application and the stable v1.3.0 tag target is unchanged.
All canonical WP0-WP5 records and historical SHA-256 ledgers remain byte-preserved.
Only three declared WP5 successor-control files are modified; all other closeout paths are additive.
The canonical WP5 SHA-256 ledger remains byte-identical to the ledger in the WP5 merge commit.
The successor SHA-256 ledger attests all controlled replacements and additive closeout surfaces.
All five closeout records validate under Draft 2020-12 schemas.
The closeout schema-bundle manifest reproduces all record and schema digests.
Hosted CI evidence records 17 successful workflows and 30 successful check jobs.
The 14 mandatory dedicated WP5 jobs remain recorded as successful.
WP5 acceptance remains 24/24 PASS and READY_FOR_WP6_AUDIT without a Gate 3 decision.
The 16-theorem Cluster B scope and T157-T162 identities remain unchanged.
Seven-role ownership, dependency DAG, WP2 semantics, WP3 lineage, and WP4 proof parity remain preserved.
No new theorem, fixture semantics, proof body, runtime outcome, or release capability is introduced.
Full Python regression and the dedicated closeout test pass.
Proof-hole scan, Lean build, and Coq build pass in disposable-worktree validation.
git diff --check passes and generated Coq dependency surfaces are restored before allowlist checks.
The synchronization helper is fast-forward-only and refuses force-push, history rewrite, or branch deletion.
No commit, push, merge, tag, GitHub Release, Zenodo deposit, DOI action, WP6 start, or Gate 3 decision is automated.
The final decision is OPEN_WP5_POST_MERGE_CLOSEOUT_PR; final development synchronization occurs only after that PR merges.
